title: The question word
description: Find the question words in these sentences!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  many of us are going?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional phrase for asking a question about numbers is **"how many"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ did you buy that chocolate lollipop?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about places is **"where"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

>I didn’t hear you;  \_\_\_\_\_\_  did you say?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about ideas and meaning is **"what"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  do you want to play outside?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about time is **"when"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  did you get those strawberries?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about places is **"where"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  are those anteaters following us?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking for an explanation is **"why"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  many inches of snow have fallen?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional phrase for asking a question about numbers is **"how many"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  happened to your hat?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about ideas and explanations is **"what"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  is Tamar’s piano recital?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about time is **"when"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  won the spelling bee?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking a question about people is **"who"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best question word for this sentence!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_  one of you jokers filled my pillowcase with shaving cream?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
The conventional word for asking to distinguish between multiple people or things is **"which"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the words in this sentence is a question word?
**

>What was Sarah eating?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
In this sentence, the question word is **"what"**.

hints: Question words ask about people (who/which), places (where), times (when), or ideas and explanations (why/what/how).
